abstract: This article examines the defining characteristics of contemporary globalization through the framework of global value chains (GVCs), deriving both positive and normative implications for the economies of Central and Eastern Europe. Specifically, we delineate the nature of the structural challenges these countries currently face and evaluate the strategic directions necessary for future public policy. Particular emphasis is placed on the role of technological advancement as a primary driver for securing high-value-added activities. These theoretical explorations are reinforced by empirical evidence, utilizing GVC integration indices and a comprehensive synthesis of current economic literature to support our findings.
